title: Crispy Egyptian Apples Tart
description: A savory, flaky tart that blends sweet apples with aromatic spices for a brunch-friendly Egyptian twist.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). Lightly grease a 9‑inch tart pan or line with parchment paper.
Flatten puff pastry on a lightly floured surface and roll to fit the pan. Press into the bottom and up the sides, trimming excess dough to leave a 1‑inch border.
In a bowl, combine the apple slices, toasted walnuts, honey, cinnamon, ginger, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Toss gently until coated.
Spread the apple mixture onto the pastry, leaving the border intact.
Brush the pastry edges with the beaten egg and sprinkle the remaining walnuts on top.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the apple filling is bubbling and slightly caramelized.
While baking, you may lightly toast additional walnuts to add extra crunch.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes. In a small bowl whisk 1 tbsp honey with 1 tsp lemon juice to create a quick glaze; drizzle over the tart.
Slice the tart into wedges and serve warm, optionally with a dollop of Greek yogurt or a sprinkle of fresh mint.
Allow the tart to rest for a couple of minutes before serving.
